name: node-spdx-expression-parse
title: node-spdx-expression-parse
description:
parse SPDX license expressions
This package parses SPDX license expression strings
describing license terms, like package.json license strings,
into consistently structured ECMAScript (JavaScript) objects.
.
SPDX license expressions are standardized string that uses
SPDX identifiers (unique and machine‑readable names for specific
software licenses) to state a project’s licensing terms clearly
using logical operators like AND, OR, and WITH.
.
This package includes small modules spdx-range, spdx-satisfies,
spdx-expression-validate and spdx-compare.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-spdx-expression-parse because parse SPDX license expressions.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-spdx-expression-parse as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-spdx-expression-parse with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-spdx-expression-parse --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
